profiles.label: Raspberry Pi / entry-level
profiles.summary: CPU-first deployment with deferred background embedding work.

scan.reason: Drop-in llama.cpp scanner that actually ships as GGUF and stays light enough for Pi-class hardware.

embedding.reason: Lighter CPU embedding path than Qwen3-Embedding-0.6B for entry-level hardware.

notes: Bias toward background indexing so live chat stays responsive.
Upgrade the embedding model first when moving off entry-level hardware.

profiles.label: Desktop / balanced
profiles.summary: Good default for mixed local and proxied workloads.

scan.reason: Keeps scanner latency low without requiring a non-llama.cpp serving stack.

embedding.reason: Better semantic recall when the host can absorb the extra footprint.

profiles.label: Workstation / GPU-heavy
profiles.summary: Keep the higher-quality local stack hot at all times.

scan.reason: Still small enough that there is no reason to waste the main chat model on redaction.

embedding.reason: Best fit when the box already has enough headroom for stronger embeddings.

notes: Use this when embedding latency is already hidden by available hardware.
